title: Exploring the relationship between poverty and homicide rates from the Mexican War on Drugs

yaml.title: Exploring the relationship between poverty and homicide rates from the Mexican War on Drugs

markdown.markdown:
This project contains the code belonging to both an honors thesis and a fulbright garcia robles fellowship while I worked at UNAM, Mexico's National University. You can read the full paper here.

```{r setup-import-data}
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(janitor)
library(tidyverse)
library(foreign)
library(haven)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(janitor)
library(stargazer)
library(kableExtra)
library(infer)
library(broom)
library(xtable)
library(DataExplorer)
install.packages("cheatsheet")
library(cheatsheet)

library(sjPlot)
library(sjmisc)
library(sjlabelled)


library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(foreign)
library(stringr)
library(MatchIt)
library(naniar)
# install.packages("arsenal")
library(arsenal)
# install.packages("vtable")
library(vtable)
#Marginalization data 
marginalization2 <- read.csv("data_raw/marg_index_06anexo_base.csv") #marg by municipality 2010, columns str are not characters
str(marginalization2)   #2463 obs. of  15 variables, variables are all correct type
sum(is.na(marginalization2)) 

#Homicide data
esochomicide <- read_dta("data_raw/ESOC_Mexico_drug_related_murders.dta") #all homicide data 2006- 11 
homicidesub <- esochomicide %>% 
    filter(year == 2010)    #subset ESOC homicide data for 2010 values
homicidesub2 <- homicidesub[,c("idunico", "deaths_total",
                                 "deaths_execution", "deaths_confront", "deaths_aggression")] 
#sum(is.na(homicidesub2))  
```

```{r dataset-merge, echo=FALSE}
#marginalization + homicides = primary df
marg_homicide_df <- read.csv("data_raw/mateo.csv") 
 marg_homicide_df_rename <- rename(marg_homicide_df, muncode = municip_code)
  sum(is.na(marg_homicide_df$deaths_total)) #checking for missing values w/ is.na,
```

```{r}
#Loading control variable data 

#Primary control variables
mex_variables <- read_dta("/Users/diegoflores/Poli_90_intro_2/Prof_Bezares_mex_vars_2010.dta") #df w controls including:km^2 of municipality, transfers (central to municipio), etc
  View(mex_variables) 
  str(mex_variables)
  summary(mex_variables)

mex_variables_2 <- subset(mex_variables, 
                          select = -c(1, 7:11, 15:21, 23:36))  #with out dummy variables 

  colSums(is.na(mex_variables_2))                       #checks total number of NA values by column 
  mex_variables_2[mex_variables_2 == 0] <- NA           #replaces ) with NA values 
                                                        #removed dummy variable in above subset in                                                            order to replace 0's with NA
  #check for dummy variables again
  colSums(is.na(mex_variables_2)) #make sure that govt transfer value of 0 means NA and not zero transfer.. it probably does so use mex variables 3
  
  # Check for Duplicates 
mex_variables_2[duplicated(mex_variables_2$muncode),] 
  duplicated(mex_variables_2$muncode) #this omits 1463 entries 
  sum(duplicated(mex_variables_2$muncode)) #0 duplicates 
  
#Most up to date df so far, 10  varibales 
mex_variables3 <- subset(mex_variables, select = -c(1, 7:11, 15:21, 23:33))  
  colSums(is.na(mex_variables3))
  #13 variables total, includes political party vars
  #mex variables3 and marg_homicide_df are principal & most up to date
  
#Govt Transfers 
mex_variables3$govt_transfer <- mex_variables$"aportaciones" + mex_variables$"participaciones"  #added new column total gov transfer to mex_variable3 da
```

```{r}
mhmerge <- merge(marginsub2, homicidesub2, by.x = c("municipality code"), by.y = c("idunico")) #merged data by INEGI code* 
table(mhmerge$deaths_total)
sum(is.na(mhmerge$deaths_total)) 
mhmerge_rename <- rename(mhmerge, municip_code = "municipality code", state_code = "state code", marg_grade = "marginalization grade", margin_index = IM, total_pop = "total population") 
# renamed variables, new df
```

```{r}
marg_homicide_df_rename$total_govt_transfer <-mex_variables3$total_govt_transfer #add govt transfer column from mexvariables3 to this df

#Road Access
road_access <- read.csv("/Users/diegoflores/Poli_90_intro_2/recovered_202203281742/unsaved/coneval_road_access.csv")
  road_access2 <- subset(road_access, 
                         select = -c(clave_entidad, 
                                     entidad_federativa, municipio,
                                     poblacion_2010))
  road_access3 <- rename(road_access2, 
                         muncode = clave_municipal, 
                         percent_road_access = porc_pob_acce_muy_baja_o_baja)
  rm(road_access)
  rm(road_access2)
  road_access3[complete.cases(road_access3), ]                 # Keep only the complete rows, removes last two 2 rows 
road_access4 <- road_access3[complete.cases(road_access3), ] # Store the complete cases subset in a new data frame
  colSums(is.na(road_access4))                                 #0 NA values. Dont replace 0s. 0's mean no percentage has difficulty                                                                  accessing paved roads 
marg_homicide_df$percent_road_access <- road_access4$percent_road_access  #add road access to primary dataframe
  rm(road_access4)  
#subset: removed repetitive column names to merge both dfs into full df 
  
# MARIJUANA SEIZURES 
marijuana_seizures <- read_dta("/Users/diegoflores/Poli_90_intro_2/Thesis_data/marihuana_1990_2016.dta")
    str(marijuana_seizures)
    summary(marijuana_seizures)
  marijuana_seizures_sub <- marijuana_seizures %>% filter(year == 2010) #filter for year 2010 
  marijuanasub2 <- subset(marijuana_seizures_sub, select = -c(id_state, state, municipio, munseat)) #remove repetitive columns 
  marijuanasub2                 #only 3 variables now: year, muncode, and seizures 
                                #add mj sub 2 to mex variables3
mex_variables3$marijuana_seizures <- marijuanasub2$marihuana

# POP DENSITY 
mex_variables3$pop_density <- mex_variables3$"population" / mex_variables3$"areakm2"  #take total population and divide by municipal measure of square kilometers

#Create homicide rate variable from deaths total
mhmerge_controls2_nj$homicide_rates10k <- (mhmerge_controls2_nj$deaths_total / mhmerge_controls2_nj$population) * 10000
mhmerge_controls2_nj$homicide_rates100k <- (mhmerge_controls2_nj$deaths_total / mhmerge_controls2_nj$population) * 100000

#IMPORTANT MERGE below #main df is mhmerge_controls2
marg_homicide_df_rename <- rename(marg_homicide_df, muncode = municip_code) #The issue is here where this df doesnt have homicide_per_100k variable instead has deaths disagregated. MAKE a homicide variable. im completely missing it. issue solved, maindf now has homicide_rates100k variable
summary(marg_homicide_df_rename)
mhmerge_controls2 <- left_join(marg_homicide_df_rename, mex_variables3, by= 'muncode') #leftjoin merge                                                                                               control to main df  
# WITHOUT OUTLIER 
mhmerge_controls2_nj <- subset(mhmerge_controls2, muncode != 8037) 
  summary(mhmerge_controls2_nj)
#NEW DF delete later
  marg_hom_deaths <- subset(mhmerge_controls2_nj, 
                            select = -c(1, 9:12, 14:16, 18, 21:23, 25:27, 30:32))
summary(marg_hom_deaths)
  marg_hom_deaths2 <- marg_hom_deaths %>% 
    arrange(desc(homicide_rates100k)) %>% 
    group_by(muncode) 
  
marg_hom_deaths2.1 <- subset(marg_hom_deaths2, 
                             select = -c(4, 10:12))

  marg_hom_deaths3 <- marg_hom_deaths %>% 
    arrange(desc(deaths_total)) %>% 
    group_by(muncode) 

#Log Transformations WITH OUTLIER 
mhmerge_controls2$loghomicide <- log(mhmerge_controls2$deaths_total + 1)
#Homicide Rates transformation w outlier 
mhmerge_controls2$homicide_rates10k <- (mhmerge_controls2$deaths_total / mhmerge_controls2$population) * 10000
mhmerge_controls2$homicide_rates100k <- (mhmerge_controls2$deaths_total / mhmerge_controls2$population) * 100000 

# NO OUTLIER
#Log tranformation of deaths total for NJ
  mhmerge_controls2_nj$loghomicide <- log(mhmerge_controls2_nj$deaths_total + 1)

#More Log transformations 
mhmerge_controls2_nj$log_govt_transfer <- log(mhmerge_controls2_nj$total_govt_transfer + 1)
mhmerge_controls2_nj$logpop_density <- log(mhmerge_controls2_nj$pop_density + 1) #also attempt to run regression with popn_10k vs pop density 
mhmerge_controls2_nj$log_percent_road_access <- log(mhmerge_controls2_nj$percent_road_access + 1)
mhmerge_controls2_nj$log_gdp_usd <- log(mhmerge_controls2_nj$gdp_usd + 1)

# New variable - region  
mhmerge_controls2_nj <- mhmerge_controls2_nj %>%  
  mutate(region = case_when(state_code %in% c(1, 6, 11, 14, 16, 18, 22, 24, 32) ~ 'central_west',
                            state_code %in% c(9, 12, 13, 15, 17, 21, 29, 20) ~ 'central',
                            state_code %in% c(2, 3, 5, 8,  10, 19, 25, 26, 28) ~ 'north',
                            state_code %in% c(4, 7, 23, 27, 30, 31) ~ 'southeast'))
 
## Summary Statistics ## 
labs <- data.frame(ogname = c('margin_index', 'deaths_total', 
                              'population','homicide_rates100k', 'marijuana_seizures'),
                  newname = c('Marginalization Index','Total Homicides',
                              'Population','Homicides per 100k', 'Marijuana Seizures'))

sumtable(mhmerge_controls2_nj, vars = c('margin_index','deaths_total', 
                                        'population','homicide_rates100k', 'marijuana_seizures'), digits = 3, labels=labs, out='htmlreturn', file ='thesis_summarystat', title = 'Table 1')
getwd()
   summ = list(
           c('notNA(x)','mean(x)','min(x)','max(x)'))
   


##Plot 1 
p1 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_controls2_nj) + 
  aes(x = margin_index, y = homicide_rates100k, color = region) +    
               geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.4) +
               labs(x = "Marginalization Index", 
                    y = "Homicide rate per 100,000",
                    title = "Marginalization Levels and Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are homicide rates per 100,000 people",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict")
p1 
p1 + ylim(0, 715) #Zooming into plot 

p1.2 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_controls2_nj) + 
  aes(x = margin_index, y = deaths_total, color = region) +    
               geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.4) +
               labs(x = "Marginalization Index", 
                    y = "Total Homicides",
                    title = "Marginalization Levels and Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are individual homicides",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict")
p1.2

#Running average - 'loess'
# Plot 1 still 
p1 + ylim(0, 715) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE)
p1 +  
  ylim(0, 400) + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2.2, 3.8)) + geom_smooth(method = "loess") + 
  geom_jitter() +
  facet_wrap(~region) 
p1 +  
  ylim(0, 715) + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2.2, 3.8)) + geom_smooth(method = "loess") + 
  geom_jitter() +
  facet_grid(region~.)
p1 +  
  ylim(0, 25) + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2.2, 3.8)) +  geom_smooth(method = "loess") +
  geom_jitter() +
  facet_wrap(.~region)

# install.packages("devEMF") # if not installed already
library(devEMF)
tmp_file <- "EMF_plot.emf"
# ?emf # if needed
emf(file= tmp_file, pointsize= 10, width= 5, height= 3) # Opens a device
temp_margins <- c(1.5, 2.3, 0.5, 0.2) # Adjust margins
par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = temp_margins,
    mgp=c(0.6, 0.3, 0),  tcl = -0.15, las = 1)
plot(p1 + ylim(0, 715) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE)) # create your plot
grid(col= "grey")
dev.off()  # Close the device

#linear 
p1 +  
  ylim(0, 600) + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2.2, 3.8)) + geom_smooth(method = "lm") + 
  facet_wrap(~region) 

#Homicides as a function of distance to US border 
p2 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_controls2_nj) + 
  aes(x = dist_us_border, y = homicide_rates100k, color = region) +    
               geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.4) +
               labs(x = "Disatance to US Border", 
                    y = "Homicide rate per 100,000",
                    title = "Distance to US Border & Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are homicide rates per 100,000 people",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict") +
                      ylim(0, 715) 
p2

#Homicide plotted against govt transfer
p3 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_controls2_nj) + 
  aes(x = log_govt_transfer, y = homicide_rates100k, color = region) +    
               geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.4) +
               labs(x = "Log Government Transfers", 
                    y = "Homicide rate per 100,000",
                    title = "Government tranfers & Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are homicide rates per 100,000 people",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict") +
                      ylim(0, 715) 
p3
#homicide plotted against population
p4 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_controls2_nj) + 
  aes(x = popn_10k, y = homicide_rates100k, color = region) +    
               geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.4) +
               labs(x = "Population per 10,000", 
                    y = "Homicide rate per 100,000",
                    title = "Population per 10,000  & Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are homicide rates per 100,000 people",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict") +
                      ylim(0, 300) + xlim(0,25)
p4
# Homicides and GDP 
p5 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_controls2_nj) + 
  aes(x = gdp_usd, y = homicide_rates100k, color = region) +    
               geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.4) +
               labs(x = "GDP (USD)", 
                    y = "Homicide rate per 100,000",
                    title = "Municipal GDP & Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are homicide rates per 100,000 people",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict") +
                      ylim(0, 250) + xlim(22000,0) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(21500,950))
p5


library(stargazer)
library(sjPlot)
library(sjmisc)
library(sjlabelled)

# W/ deaths total & outlier 
model_outlier <- lm(deaths_total ~ margin_index, data  = marg_homicide_df)
summary(model_outlier)

# W/ homicide rate & outlier 
model_outlier2 <- lm(homicide_rates100k ~ margin_index, data = mhmerge_controls2)
summary(model_outlier2)

#Base model - no outlier, no fixed effect
model1 <- lm(homicide_rates100k ~ margin_index , data = mhmerge_controls2_nj)
summary(model1)
confint(model1)

#hist(resid(model1))
#qqnorm(resid(model1)) 
#qqline(resid(model1))
# Creating table
#stargazer(basemodel1) # disable stargazer messages in header - isnt working 

#BASE model - simple regression, no outlier, fixed effect
model2 <- lm(homicide_rates100k ~ margin_index + as.factor(state_code), data = mhmerge_controls2_nj)
summary(model2)
confint(model2)

# Multiple regression - no outlier
         
model3 <- lm(homicide_rates100k ~ margin_index + log_govt_transfer + popn_10k + log_percent_road_access + log_gdp_usd + as.factor(state_code), data = mhmerge_controls2_nj)
summary(model3)
confint(model3)

model4  <-  lm(homicide_rates100k ~ margin_index + log_govt_transfer + popn_10k + log_percent_road_access + log_gdp_usd + marijuana_seizures + same_all + same_state_mun + same_mun_exec + as.factor(state_code), data = mhmerge_controls2_nj)
summary(model4)
confint(model4)

model5  <-  lm(homicide_rates100k ~ margin_index +log_govt_transfer + popn_10k + log_percent_road_access + log_gdp_usd + marijuana_seizures + same_all + same_state_mun + same_mun_exec, data = mhmerge_controls2_nj)
summary(model5) #multiple regression, no state FE 
confint(model5)


stargazer(model1, model2, model3, model4, model5, type = 'text')

#Using Sjplot 
tab_model(model1, model2, model3, model4, model5) 

# regression output in html with output saved to file, good for word
stargazer(model1, model2, model3, model4, model5, type = 'html', out = 'my_regression_table.html')
stargazer(attitude)
```
markdown.srcMarkdownNoYaml:
This project contains the code belonging to both an honors thesis and a fulbright garcia robles fellowship while I worked at UNAM, Mexico's National University. You can read the full paper here.

```{r setup-import-data}
library(readr)
library(ggplot2)
library(janitor)
library(tidyverse)
library(foreign)
library(haven)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(janitor)
library(stargazer)
library(kableExtra)
library(infer)
library(broom)
library(xtable)
# install.packages(DataExplorer) 
library(DataExplorer)
install.packages("cheatsheet")
library(cheatsheet)

library(sjPlot)
library(sjmisc)
library(sjlabelled)


library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(foreign)
library(stringr)
library(MatchIt)
library(naniar)
# install.packages("arsenal")
library(arsenal)
# install.packages("vtable")
library(vtable)
#Marginalization data 
marginalization2 <- read.csv("data_raw/marg_index_06anexo_base.csv") #marg by municipality 2010, columns str are not characters
str(marginalization2)   #2463 obs. of  15 variables, variables are all correct type
sum(is.na(marginalization2)) 

#Homicide data
esochomicide <- read_dta("data_raw/ESOC_Mexico_drug_related_murders.dta") #all homicide data 2006- 11 
homicidesub <- esochomicide %>% 
    filter(year == 2010)    #subset ESOC homicide data for 2010 values
homicidesub2 <- homicidesub[,c("idunico", "deaths_total",
                                 "deaths_execution", "deaths_confront", "deaths_aggression")] 
#sum(is.na(homicidesub2))  
```

```{r dataset-merge, echo=FALSE}
#marginalization + homicides = primary df
marg_homicide_df <- read.csv("data_raw/mateo.csv") 
 marg_homicide_df_rename <- rename(marg_homicide_df, muncode = municip_code)
  sum(is.na(marg_homicide_df$deaths_total)) #checking for missing values w/ is.na,
```

```{r}
#Loading control variable data 

#Primary control variables
mex_variables <- read_dta("/Users/diegoflores/Poli_90_intro_2/Prof_Bezares_mex_vars_2010.dta") #df w controls including:km^2 of municipality, transfers (central to municipio), etc
  View(mex_variables) 
  str(mex_variables)
  summary(mex_variables)

mex_variables_2 <- subset(mex_variables, 
                          select = -c(1, 7:11, 15:21, 23:36))  #with out dummy variables 

  colSums(is.na(mex_variables_2))                       #checks total number of NA values by column 
  mex_variables_2[mex_variables_2 == 0] <- NA           #replaces ) with NA values 
                                                        #removed dummy variable in above subset in                                                            order to replace 0's with NA
  #check for dummy variables again
  colSums(is.na(mex_variables_2)) #make sure that govt transfer value of 0 means NA and not zero transfer.. it probably does so use mex variables 3
  
  # Check for Duplicates 
mex_variables_2[duplicated(mex_variables_2$muncode),] 
  duplicated(mex_variables_2$muncode) #this omits 1463 entries 
  sum(duplicated(mex_variables_2$muncode)) #0 duplicates 
  
#Most up to date df so far, 10  varibales 
mex_variables3 <- subset(mex_variables, select = -c(1, 7:11, 15:21, 23:33))  
  colSums(is.na(mex_variables3))
  #13 variables total, includes political party vars
  #mex variables3 and marg_homicide_df are principal & most up to date
  
#Govt Transfers 
mex_variables3$govt_transfer <- mex_variables$"aportaciones" + mex_variables$"participaciones"  #added new column total gov transfer to mex_variable3 da
```

```{r}
mhmerge <- merge(marginsub2, homicidesub2, by.x = c("municipality code"), by.y = c("idunico")) #merged data by INEGI code* 
table(mhmerge$deaths_total)
sum(is.na(mhmerge$deaths_total))
```

```{r}

#Road Access
road_access <- read.csv("/Users/diegoflores/Poli_90_intro_2/recovered_202203281742/unsaved/coneval_road_access.csv")
  road_access2 <- subset(road_access, 
                         select = -c(clave_entidad, 
                                     entidad_federativa, municipio,
                                     poblacion_2010))
  road_access3 <- rename(road_access2, 
                         muncode = clave_municipal, 
                         percent_road_access = porc_pob_acce_muy_baja_o_baja)
  rm(road_access)
  rm(road_access2)
  road_access3[complete.cases(road_access3), ]                 # Keep only the complete rows, removes last two 2 rows 
road_access4 <- road_access3[complete.cases(road_access3), ] # Store the complete cases subset in a new data frame
  colSums(is.na(road_access4))                                 #0 NA values. Dont replace 0s. 0's mean no percentage has difficulty                                                                  accessing paved roads 
marg_homicide_df$percent_road_access <- road_access4$percent_road_access  #add road access to primary dataframe
  rm(road_access4)  
#subset: removed repetitive column names to merge both dfs into full df 
  
# MARIJUANA SEIZURES 
marijuana_seizures <- read_dta("/Users/diegoflores/Poli_90_intro_2/Thesis_data/marihuana_1990_2016.dta")
    str(marijuana_seizures)
    summary(marijuana_seizures)
  marijuana_seizures_sub <- marijuana_seizures %>% filter(year == 2010) #filter for year 2010 
  marijuanasub2 <- subset(marijuana_seizures_sub, select = -c(id_state, state, municipio, munseat)) #remove repetitive columns 
  marijuanasub2                 #only 3 variables now: year, muncode, and seizures 
                                #add mj sub 2 to mex variables3
mex_variables3$marijuana_seizures <- marijuanasub2$marihuana

# POP DENSITY 
mex_variables3$pop_density <- mex_variables3$"population" / mex_variables3$"areakm2"  #take total population and divide by municipal measure of square kilometers

#Create homicide rate variable from deaths total
mhmerge_controls2_nj$homicide_rates10k <- (mhmerge_controls2_nj$deaths_total / mhmerge_controls2_nj$population) * 10000
mhmerge_controls2_nj$homicide_rates100k <- (mhmerge_controls2_nj$deaths_total / mhmerge_controls2_nj$population) * 100000

#IMPORTANT MERGE below #main df is mhmerge_controls2
marg_homicide_df_rename <- rename(marg_homicide_df, muncode = municip_code) #The issue is here where this df doesnt have homicide_per_100k variable instead has deaths disagregated. MAKE a homicide variable. im completely missing it. issue solved, maindf now has homicide_rates100k variable
summary(marg_homicide_df_rename)
mhmerge_controls2 <- left_join(marg_homicide_df_rename, mex_variables3, by= 'muncode') #leftjoin merge                                                                                               control to main df  
# WITHOUT OUTLIER 
mhmerge_controls2_nj <- subset(mhmerge_controls2, muncode != 8037) 
  summary(mhmerge_controls2_nj)
#NEW DF delete later
  marg_hom_deaths <- subset(mhmerge_controls2_nj, 
                            select = -c(1, 9:12, 14:16, 18, 21:23, 25:27, 30:32))
summary(marg_hom_deaths)
  marg_hom_deaths2 <- marg_hom_deaths %>% 
    arrange(desc(homicide_rates100k)) %>% 
    group_by(muncode) 
  
marg_hom_deaths2.1 <- subset(marg_hom_deaths2, 
                             select = -c(4, 10:12))

  marg_hom_deaths3 <- marg_hom_deaths %>% 
    arrange(desc(deaths_total)) %>% 
    group_by(muncode) 

#Log Transformations WITH OUTLIER 
mhmerge_controls2$loghomicide <- log(mhmerge_controls2$deaths_total + 1)
#Homicide Rates transformation w outlier 
mhmerge_controls2$homicide_rates10k <- (mhmerge_controls2$deaths_total / mhmerge_controls2$population) * 10000
mhmerge_controls2$homicide_rates100k <- (mhmerge_controls2$deaths_total / mhmerge_controls2$population) * 100000 

# NO OUTLIER
#Log tranformation of deaths total for NJ
  mhmerge_controls2_nj$loghomicide <- log(mhmerge_controls2_nj$deaths_total + 1)

#More Log transformations 
mhmerge_controls2_nj$log_govt_transfer <- log(mhmerge_controls2_nj$total_govt_transfer + 1)
mhmerge_controls2_nj$logpop_density <- log(mhmerge_controls2_nj$pop_density + 1) #also attempt to run regression with popn_10k vs pop density 
mhmerge_controls2_nj$log_percent_road_access <- log(mhmerge_controls2_nj$percent_road_access + 1)
mhmerge_controls2_nj$log_gdp_usd <- log(mhmerge_controls2_nj$gdp_usd + 1)

# New variable - region  
mhmerge_controls2_nj <- mhmerge_controls2_nj %>%  
  mutate(region = case_when(state_code %in% c(1, 6, 11, 14, 16, 18, 22, 24, 32) ~ 'central_west',
                            state_code %in% c(9, 12, 13, 15, 17, 21, 29, 20) ~ 'central',
                            state_code %in% c(2, 3, 5, 8,  10, 19, 25, 26, 28) ~ 'north',
                            state_code %in% c(4, 7, 23, 27, 30, 31) ~ 'southeast'))
 
## Summary Statistics ## 
labs <- data.frame(ogname = c('margin_index', 'deaths_total', 
                              'population','homicide_rates100k', 'marijuana_seizures'),
                  newname = c('Marginalization Index','Total Homicides',
                              'Population','Homicides per 100k', 'Marijuana Seizures'))

sumtable(mhmerge_controls2_nj, vars = c('margin_index','deaths_total', 
                                        'population','homicide_rates100k', 'marijuana_seizures'), digits = 3, labels=labs, out='htmlreturn', file ='thesis_summarystat', title = 'Table 1')
getwd()
   summ = list(
           c('notNA(x)','mean(x)','min(x)','max(x)'))
   


##Plot 1 
p1 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_controls2_nj) + 
  aes(x = margin_index, y = homicide_rates100k, color = region) +    
               geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.4) +
               labs(x = "Marginalization Index", 
                    y = "Homicide rate per 100,000",
                    title = "Marginalization Levels and Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are homicide rates per 100,000 people",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict")
p1 
p1 + ylim(0, 715) #Zooming into plot 

p1.2 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_controls2_nj) + 
  aes(x = margin_index, y = deaths_total, color = region) +    
               geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.4) +
               labs(x = "Marginalization Index", 
                    y = "Total Homicides",
                    title = "Marginalization Levels and Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are individual homicides",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict")
p1.2

#Running average - 'loess'
# Plot 1 still 
p1 + ylim(0, 715) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE)
p1 +  
  ylim(0, 400) + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2.2, 3.8)) + geom_smooth(method = "loess") + 
  geom_jitter() +
  facet_wrap(~region) 
p1 +  
  ylim(0, 715) + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2.2, 3.8)) + geom_smooth(method = "loess") + 
  geom_jitter() +
  facet_grid(region~.)
p1 +  
  ylim(0, 25) + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2.2, 3.8)) +  geom_smooth(method = "loess") +
  geom_jitter() +
  facet_wrap(.~region)

# install.packages("devEMF") # if not installed already
library(devEMF)
tmp_file <- "EMF_plot.emf"
# ?emf # if needed
emf(file= tmp_file, pointsize= 10, width= 5, height= 3) # Opens a device
temp_margins <- c(1.5, 2.3, 0.5, 0.2) # Adjust margins
par(mfrow = c(1,1), mar = temp_margins,
    mgp=c(0.6, 0.3, 0),  tcl = -0.15, las = 1)
plot(p1 + ylim(0, 715) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE)) # create your plot
grid(col= "grey")
dev.off()  # Close the device

#linear 
p1 +  
  ylim(0, 600) + coord_cartesian(xlim = c(-2.2, 3.8)) + geom_smooth(method = "lm") + 
  facet_wrap(~region) 

#Homicides as a function of distance to US border 
p2 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_controls2_nj) + 
  aes(x = dist_us_border, y = homicide_rates100k, color = region) +    
               geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.4) +
               labs(x = "Disatance to US Border", 
                    y = "Homicide rate per 100,000",
                    title = "Distance to US Border & Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are homicide rates per 100,000 people",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict") +
                      ylim(0, 715) 
p2

#Homicide plotted against govt transfer
p3 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_controls2_nj) + 
  aes(x = log_govt_transfer, y = homicide_rates100k, color = region) +    
               geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.4) +
               labs(x = "Log Government Transfers", 
                    y = "Homicide rate per 100,000",
                    title = "Government tranfers & Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are homicide rates per 100,000 people",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict") +
                      ylim(0, 715) 
p3
#homicide plotted against population
p4 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_controls2_nj) + 
  aes(x = popn_10k, y = homicide_rates100k, color = region) +    
               geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.4) +
               labs(x = "Population per 10,000", 
                    y = "Homicide rate per 100,000",
                    title = "Population per 10,000  & Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are homicide rates per 100,000 people",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict") +
                      ylim(0, 300) + xlim(0,25)
p4
# Homicides and GDP 
p5 <- ggplot(data = mhmerge_controls2_nj) + 
  aes(x = gdp_usd, y = homicide_rates100k, color = region) +    
               geom_point(size = 1.4, alpha = 0.4) +
               labs(x = "GDP (USD)", 
                    y = "Homicide rate per 100,000",
                    title = "Municipal GDP & Drug War Homicides", 
                    subtitle = "Data points are homicide rates per 100,000 people",
                    caption = "Source: Princeton Empirical Studies of Conflict") +
                      ylim(0, 250) + xlim(22000,0) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(21500,950))
p5


library(stargazer)
library(sjPlot)
library(sjmisc)
library(sjlabelled)

# W/ deaths total & outlier 
model_outlier <- lm(deaths_total ~ margin_index, data  = marg_homicide_df)
summary(model_outlier)

# W/ homicide rate & outlier 
model_outlier2 <- lm(homicide_rates100k ~ margin_index, data = mhmerge_controls2)
summary(model_outlier2)

#Base model - no outlier, no fixed effect
model1 <- lm(homicide_rates100k ~ margin_index , data = mhmerge_controls2_nj)
summary(model1)
confint(model1)

#hist(resid(model1))
#qqnorm(resid(model1)) 
#qqline(resid(model1))
# Creating table
#stargazer(basemodel1) # disable stargazer messages in header - isnt working 

#BASE model - simple regression, no outlier, fixed effect
model2 <- lm(homicide_rates100k ~ margin_index + as.factor(state_code), data = mhmerge_controls2_nj)
summary(model2)
confint(model2)

# Multiple regression - no outlier
         
model3 <- lm(homicide_rates100k ~ margin_index + log_govt_transfer + popn_10k + log_percent_road_access + log_gdp_usd + as.factor(state_code), data = mhmerge_controls2_nj)
summary(model3)
confint(model3)

model4  <-  lm(homicide_rates100k ~ margin_index + log_govt_transfer + popn_10k + log_percent_road_access + log_gdp_usd + marijuana_seizures + same_all + same_state_mun + same_mun_exec + as.factor(state_code), data = mhmerge_controls2_nj)
summary(model4)
confint(model4)

model5  <-  lm(homicide_rates100k ~ margin_index +log_govt_transfer + popn_10k + log_percent_road_access + log_gdp_usd + marijuana_seizures + same_all + same_state_mun + same_mun_exec, data = mhmerge_controls2_nj)
summary(model5) #multiple regression, no state FE 
confint(model5)


stargazer(model1, model2, model3, model4, model5, type = 'text')

#Using Sjplot 
tab_model(model1, model2, model3, model4, model5) 

# regression output in html with output saved to file, good for word
stargazer(model1, model2, model3, model4, model5, type = 'html', out = 'my_regression_table.html')
stargazer(attitude)
```
